raw_consumer_statement: I was a victim of fraud, someone opened a acct XX/XX/XXXX, and it was connected to my acct. Was not notified until XX/XX/XXXX. I notified Macys of fraud XX/XX/XXXX. They did reverse the charges on the fraud accout ( I didn't know it was not my acct, when I opened XXXX the fraudulent account was primary acct ). After the acct was zeroed ( XXXX ) out I purchased a pair of slippers ( XXXX ) and paid immediately. On XXXX I rec 'd notice Macys, stating that I am responsible for the charges made on fraudulent acct. However, in XXXX of XXXX they added the fraudulent charges to my account. I ended up being sued by Citibank for XXXX and now have a lien on my property. The judge stated that had it been filed in Small Claims and knew the opening of my acct. I would have won. I don't get that. Macys had my orginal account was opened in XXXX and that it was close to the opening of fraudulent acct. Crazy. I did contact Macys asked for history of my acct and it was opened XXXX. I could find it but not them. This issue has been going on for XXXX years. I have plenty of documentation showing that the scammer changed my address twice, I have 5 letters from different businesses where they tried to open other accts. They knew it's not my debt, they are just ugly. Im a senior citizen living off of SS and suffer from XXXX. I represented myself due to lack of $ . The court wasn't interested in any of the documentation that I had. It was a bench trial. I was victimized by the courts. FYI I also had police report.
